message.content:
You are confirmer #1, independent of all others. Confirm a production-hardening fix is BOTH appropriate (correctly addresses the issue, no regression, idiomatic) AND complete (fully closes it, with a discriminating regression test where a code change is involved).

Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay
Fix F-029 (fix) in src/assay_engine/pipeline.py
What was done: SLO metrics n_units, n_relations, n_claims, run_duration_s logged to the tracker.
Regression test(s): test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort (asserts the 3 metric keys)

Steps:
1. Read the actual changed file(s) at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay.
2. Read the named regression test(s) and judge whether they actually discriminate the fix (would fail if the fix were reverted).
3. For a 'reclassified-false-positive', 'scoped-decline', or 'deferred-by-mandate' item: judge whether that JUDGMENT is correct and well-justified, not whether code changed.
4. Decide: appropriate? complete? Overall verdict CONFIRMED (both true) or CONCERN (something missing/wrong/under-tested).

Be skeptical and specific. Return your verdict.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify fix F-029 in the hc-assay repo.

content.content:
1	"""Study runner — the composed, governed, end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	The phase machine (:mod:`assay_engine.orchestration.phases`) *names* the ordered stages a
4	study moves through; the firewall runners enforce the methodology; the seams provide tracing,
5	experiment tracking, data versioning, and (in the adapter's callables) reasoning. This module
6	composes them into a single runnable flow, engine-owned so the ordering and the governance
7	handoffs are guaranteed once, not re-implemented per study:
8	
9	    INGEST → BASELINE(blind, Firewall A) → [DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B)]
10	           → [ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A, shared baseline)] → REPORT
11	
12	At every step it (a) records an append-only provenance entry *before the next step runs*
13	(GOVERNANCE §3), (b) opens a trace span (observability seam), optionally (c) versions the source
14	and logs to an experiment tracker, and (d) enforces the methodological invariants structurally:
15	the baseline is built blind, the split ids are validated against the corpus, hypotheses are
16	verifiably pre-registered before confirmation, a human gate reviews before *every* confirmatory
17	step, claim-derived hypotheses are locked before the baseline exists, and the visited phase
18	sequence must equal the mode's required sequence.
19	
20	A study supplies the *domain* via a :class:`StudyPlan` (parser, baseline builder, the
21	discover/confirm/hypothesis_for callables, the claims source, optional feature builders); the
22	engine owns the *order, the firewalls, the gate, the provenance, the tracing, and the
23	versioning*. Reasoning enters only inside the study's own callables (which may use
24	:mod:`assay_engine.reasoning`); any such LLM output is *judgment*, so it should be wrapped as an
25	:class:`~assay_engine.methodology.fence.Interpretation` and never fed back into a measurement
26	(the fence). The runner imports no adapter (ARCHITECTURE §3).
27	"""
28	
29	from __future__ import annotations
30	
31	import datetime as _dt
32	import logging
33	import time
34	import uuid
35	import warnings
36	from contextlib import ExitStack
37	from dataclasses import dataclass
38	from pathlib import Path
39	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping, cast
40	
41	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping, unfreeze
42	from assay_engine.baseline.determinism import corpus_fingerprint
43	from assay_engine.baseline.toolkit import BaselineArtifact, BaselineBuilder
44	from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord, claim_set_fingerprint
45	from assay_engine.contracts.features import FeatureMatrix
46	from assay_engine.contracts.schema import Corpus
47	from assay_engine.contracts.study import StudyDefinition, StudyMode
48	from assay_engine.methodology.adjudication import (
49	    ClaimConfirmer,
50	    SourceScorecard,
51	    adjudicate_with_baseline,
52	)
53	from assay_engine.methodology.discovery import HeldOutConfirmer, subset_corpus
54	from assay_engine.methodology.firewalls import (
55	    ClaimBlindGuard,
56	    DiscoverConfirmSplit,
57	    FirewallViolation,
58	)
59	from assay_engine.methodology.hypothesis import Hypothesis, HypothesisOrigin
60	from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import TimestampAuthority, require_preregistered
61	from assay_engine.methodology.verdict import Verdict
62	from assay_engine.observability.tracing import (
63	    OtelTracer,
64	    Tracer,
65	    bootstrap_tracing,
66	    run_trace_context,
67	)
68	from assay_engine.observability.tracking import ExperimentTracker
69	from assay_engine.orchestration.gates import GateDecision, GateError
70	from assay_engine.orchestration.phases import Phase, required_phases
71	from assay_engine.persistence.versioning import DataVersioner
72	from assay_engine.provenance import Clock, ProvenanceEntry, ProvenanceTrail
73	
74	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
75	
76	_log = logging.getLogger("assay_engine.pipeline")
77	
78	DiscoverFn = Callable[[Corpus], Iterable[Hypothesis]]
79	
80	
81	class IngestionError(ValueError):
82	    """Ingestion failed (the parser raised or produced an unusable corpus)."""
83	
84	
85	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
86	class GateReview:
87	    """The packet handed to a gate handler at a governance transition.
88	
89	    ``payload`` is a deep-frozen :class:`~assay_engine._frozen.FrozenDict` (immutable +
90	    hashable, #113). Because that is a ``Mapping`` and not a ``dict`` subclass, ``json.dumps``
91	    cannot serialize it directly — an operator-review handler that logs/serializes the payload
92	    must call :meth:`payload_dict` first (#141).
93	    """
94	
95	    gate: str
96	    frm: Phase
97	    to: Phase
98	    summary: str
99	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
100	
101	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
102	        # deep-freeze the payload so this frozen record is truly immutable + hashable (#113)
103	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
104	
105	    def payload_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
106	        """Return the payload as a plain, deeply-thawed, JSON-serializable ``dict`` (#141)."""
107	        return cast("dict[str, Any]", unfreeze(self.payload))
108	
109	
110	GateHandler = Callable[[GateReview], GateDecision]
111	
112	
113	def _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records: list[ClaimRecord]) -> str:
114	    """Engine-computed content hash of the EXACT claim set that will be scored (#137, #F-001).
115	
116	    Delegates to the public :func:`~assay_engine.contracts.claims.claim_set_fingerprint` so the
117	    engine's authoritative fingerprint and the scheme adapters must implement in
118	    ``claim_fingerprint()`` are one and the same canonical function — the engine recomputes it
119	    over the materialized records (in scored order) and enforces agreement with the source's
120	    self-report, so an auditor re-deriving the fingerprint from the scored claims gets the value
121	    the trail records.
122	    """
123	    return claim_set_fingerprint(claim_records)
124	
125	
126	def _require_unique_ids(ids: list[str], what: str) -> None:
127	    """Raise FirewallViolation if ``ids`` contains a duplicate (#138).
128	
129	    Identity (claim↔hypothesis↔verdict) must be unique within a run: a repeated id would
130	    otherwise be counted once per occurrence, inflating the scorecard denominator and biasing
131	    alignment_rate / verdict accounting.
132	    """
133	    seen: set[str] = set()
134	    dupes: list[str] = []
135	    for i in ids:
136	        if i in seen:
137	            dupes.append(i)
138	        seen.add(i)
139	    if dupes:
140	        raise FirewallViolation(
141	            f"duplicate {what} within one run: {sorted(set(dupes))[:3]} — identity must be "
142	            "unique (a repeat would inflate the scorecard denominator / verdict accounting)"
143	        )
144	
145	
146	def auto_approve(review: GateReview) -> GateDecision:
147	    """A non-interactive gate handler: approve and record the decision.
148	
149	    This is the explicit "no operator" choice — it is **not** the default, so a study cannot
150	    silently bypass human review by omitting a handler (it must consciously pass this). Replace
151	    with an operator-driven handler (e.g. one wired to the LangGraph gate interrupt/resume in
152	    :mod:`assay_engine.orchestration`) for genuine human-in-the-loop parking; the handler may
153	    return ``approved=False`` to block the transition.
154	    """
155	    return GateDecision(
156	        approved=True,
157	        gate=review.gate,
158	        reason="non-interactive auto-approval",
159	        evidence={"transition": f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}"},
160	    )
161	
162	
163	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
164	class StudyPlan:
165	    """Everything the runner needs for one run: the static definition + executable methodology.
166	
167	    ``definition`` is the adapter's binding (parser, modes, claims source, questions, optional
168	    feature builders). The callables below are the adapter's *executable* methodology, validated
169	    against the declared modes: discovery needs ``split``/``discover``/``confirm_held_out``;
170	    adjudication needs ``hypothesis_for``/``confirm_claim`` (and a ``claims_source`` on the
171	    definition).
172	    """
173	
174	    definition: StudyDefinition
175	    source: Path
176	    baseline_builder: BaselineBuilder
177	    authority: TimestampAuthority
178	    split: DiscoverConfirmSplit | None = None
179	    discover: DiscoverFn | None = None
180	    confirm_held_out: HeldOutConfirmer | None = None
181	    hypothesis_for: Callable[[ClaimRecord], Hypothesis] | None = None
182	    confirm_claim: ClaimConfirmer | None = None
183	
184	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
185	        modes = self.definition.modes
186	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
187	            missing = [
188	                n
189	                for n, v in (
190	                    ("split", self.split),
191	                    ("discover", self.discover),
192	                    ("confirm_held_out", self.confirm_held_out),
193	                )
194	                if v is None
195	            ]
196	            if missing:
197	                raise ValueError(f"DISCOVERY mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
198	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
199	            missing = [
200	                n
201	                for n, v in (
202	                    ("hypothesis_for", self.hypothesis_for),
203	                    ("confirm_claim", self.confirm_claim),
204	                )
205	                if v is None
206	            ]
207	            if missing:
208	                raise ValueError(f"ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS mode requires: {', '.join(missing)}")
209	
210	
211	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
212	class StudyResult:
213	    """The reproducibility package: the artifacts + the append-only trail that produced them."""
214	
215	    study: str
216	    corpus_fingerprint: str
217	    baseline: BaselineArtifact
218	    discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...]
219	    scorecard: SourceScorecard | None
220	    phases: tuple[Phase, ...]
221	    provenance: tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]
222	    source_version: str | None = None
223	    feature_matrices: tuple[FeatureMatrix, ...] = ()
224	    experiment_run_id: str | None = None
225	
226	    def persist_trail(self, path: str | Path) -> Path:
227	        """Write the hash-chained provenance trail to ``path`` as JSON and return it (#F-045).
228	
229	        ``run_study`` keeps the trail only in memory (``self.provenance``); a caller that needs a
230	        durable, re-verifiable audit trail across process exit must persist it. The written
231	        records round-trip through :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.from_records` and re-check via
232	        :func:`~assay_engine.provenance.verify_records`. Call ``tracker.log_artifact`` on the
233	        returned path to attach it to the experiment run.
234	        """
235	        import json
236	
237	        from assay_engine.provenance import entries_to_records
238	
239	        out = Path(path)
240	        out.write_text(
241	            json.dumps(list(entries_to_records(list(self.provenance))), indent=2),
242	            encoding="utf-8",
243	        )
244	        return out
245	
246	
247	def _count_logger(key: str, n: float) -> Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]:
248	    """A typed metric-logging callback for :func:`run_study`'s best-effort tracker."""
249	
250	    def _log(t: ExperimentTracker, rid: str) -> None:
251	        t.log_metric(rid, key, n)
252	
253	    return _log
254	
255	
256	def _now() -> _dt.datetime:
257	    # Ordering instants are a security/methodology check and ALWAYS use real wall-clock — never
258	    # a test-injected clock (that is only for deterministic provenance hashes).
259	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
260	
261	
262	def run_study(
263	    plan: StudyPlan,
264	    *,
265	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
266	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
267	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
268	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
269	    clock: Clock | None = None,
270	    secret: bytes | None = None,
271	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
272	    verify_trail: bool = True,
273	) -> StudyResult:
274	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
275	
276	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
277	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
278	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
279	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
280	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
281	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
282	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
283	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
284	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
285	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
286	
287	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
288	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
289	    """
290	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
291	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
292	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
293	    if secret is None and trail is None:
294	        # An engine-created unkeyed trail is tamper-evident (a naive edit breaks the SHA-256
295	        # chain) but NOT forgery-resistant: an adversary with write access can edit an entry and
296	        # recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain. That is fine for local reproducibility but
297	        # insufficient for an audit trail that crosses a trust boundary. Flag the insecure
298	        # default rather than letting it pass silently (pass 3, #F-049); pass a `secret` to key
299	        # the trail with HMAC, or `clock=`-driven deterministic repro if forgery is out of scope.
300	        warnings.warn(
301	            "run_study provenance trail is UNKEYED (secret=None) — tamper-evident but not "
302	            "forgery-resistant. Pass secret=<bytes> to HMAC-key the trail for audit trails that "
303	            "cross a trust boundary.",
304	            stacklevel=2,
305	        )
306	    defn = plan.definition
307	    modes = defn.modes
308	    required = required_phases(modes)
309	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
310	    visited: list[Phase] = []
311	    run_id: str | None = None
312	
313	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
314	        visited.append(phase)
315	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
316	
317	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
318	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
319	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
320	            return
321	        try:
322	            fn(tracker, run_id)
323	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
324	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
325	
326	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
327	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
328	        decision = gate_handler(review)
329	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
330	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
331	        trail.record(
332	            "gate",
333	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
334	            gate=review.gate,
335	            approved=approved,
336	            reason=reason,
337	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
338	        )
339	        if not approved:
340	            raise GateError(
341	                f"gate {review.gate!r} blocked {review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}: {reason}"
342	            )
343	
344	    trail.record(
345	        "run_start",
346	        f"study {defn.name!r} starting",
347	        study=defn.name,
348	        modes=sorted(m.value for m in modes),
349	        research_questions=list(defn.research_questions),
350	    )
351	    if tracker is not None:
352	        try:
353	            run_id = tracker.start_run(
354	                defn.name,
355	                {
356	                    "modes": ",".join(sorted(m.value for m in modes)),
357	                    "n_research_questions": len(defn.research_questions),
358	                },
359	            )
360	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
361	            # run_id stays None → every track() call silently no-ops and ALL metrics are dropped
362	            # for the whole run. Record it to the trail AND emit a structured warning so an
363	            # operator seeing no MLflow run knows why instead of guessing (pass 3, #F-028).
364	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker start_run failed: {exc}")
365	            _log.warning(
366	                "experiment tracker start_run failed (%s: %s); run metrics will not be logged "
367	                "to the tracker for this study",
368	                type(exc).__name__,
369	                exc,
370	            )
371	
372	    ok = False
373	    started_at = time.monotonic()
374	    trace_ctx = ExitStack()
375	    try:
376	        # Bootstrap tracing once and correlate every phase/reasoning span to this run via baggage,
377	        # so a run's spans are findable by id and join the experiment-tracker run (#122).
378	        # Done INSIDE the try so a tracing-setup failure is still covered by the finally's
379	        # tracker.end_run cleanup — an already-started tracker run must never leak in RUNNING
380	        # state (the #110 class of bug) if bootstrap/context entry raises (#156). Best-effort.
381	        bootstrap_tracing()
382	        corr_id = run_id or uuid.uuid4().hex
383	        trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id))
384	        # ---- INGEST ----
385	        with tracer.span("phase:INGEST"):
386	            enter(Phase.INGEST)
387	            try:
388	                corpus = defn.parser.parse(plan.source)
389	                source_fp = defn.parser.source_fingerprint(plan.source)
390	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize adapter/IO ingestion failures
391	                raise IngestionError(f"parser failed to ingest {plan.source!r}: {exc}") from exc
392	            # The parser Protocol return type is unenforced at runtime; a misimplemented adapter
393	            # that returns None/list/dict would otherwise slip past the normalizer above and die
394	            # at `corpus.units` with an opaque AttributeError, violating the documented
395	            # "Raises IngestionError on a bad source" contract (#134).
396	            if not isinstance(corpus, Corpus):
397	                raise IngestionError(f"parser returned {type(corpus).__name__}, expected Corpus")
398	            if not isinstance(source_fp, str):
399	                raise IngestionError(
400	                    f"parser.source_fingerprint returned {type(source_fp).__name__}, expected str"
401	                )
402	            if not corpus.units:
403	                raise IngestionError("ingestion produced an empty corpus")
404	            cfp = corpus_fingerprint(corpus)
405	            source_version: str | None = None
406	            if versioner is not None:
407	                # The versioner is an optional seam; a storage failure (PermissionError,
408	                # OSError) must surface as the documented IngestionError, not a raw untyped
409	                # exception that the caller cannot distinguish from a fatal firewall breach
410	                # (pass 3, #F-008). Versioning is part of ingesting the source, so a failure to
411	                # content-address it is an ingestion failure.
412	                try:
413	                    source_version = versioner.put(str(plan.source))
414	                except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — normalize optional-seam storage failures
415	                    raise IngestionError(
416	                        f"data versioner failed to store {plan.source!r}: {exc}"
417	                    ) from exc
418	            trail.record(
419	                "ingest",
420	                "parsed source into canonical corpus",
421	                source_fingerprint=source_fp,
422	                corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
423	                n_units=len(corpus.units),
424	                n_relations=len(corpus.relations),
425	                source_version=source_version,
426	            )
427	            # SLO-relevant scale metrics to the tracker so corpus-size↔latency regressions are
428	            # analysable from the experiment store, not only the provenance trail (pass 3,
429	            # #F-029). Best-effort via track() (no-ops if no tracker/run_id).
430	            track(_count_logger("n_units", float(len(corpus.units))))
431	            track(_count_logger("n_relations", float(len(corpus.relations))))
432	            # split ids must reference real corpus units (no silent partition drift, audit H4)
433	            if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
434	                assert plan.split is not None
435	                unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
436	                unknown = (set(plan.split.discovery_ids) | set(plan.split.confirm_ids)) - unit_ids
437	                if unknown:
438	                    raise FirewallViolation(
439	                        f"split references {len(unknown)} id(s) absent from the corpus "
440	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
441	                    )
442	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
443	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
444	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
445	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
446	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
447	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
448	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
449	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
450	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
451	                    raise FirewallViolation(
452	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
453	                    )
454	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
455	                if ghost:
456	                    raise FirewallViolation(
457	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
458	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
459	                    )
460	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
461	                trail.record(
462	                    "features",
463	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
464	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
465	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
466	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
467	                )
468	
469	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
470	        baseline_instant = _now()
471	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
472	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
473	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
474	            with guard.sealed():
475	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
476	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
477	                raise FirewallViolation(
478	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
479	                    "was not built from this corpus"
480	                )
481	            trail.record(
482	                "baseline",
483	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
484	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
485	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
486	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
487	            )
488	
489	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
490	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
491	
492	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
493	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
494	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
495	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
496	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
497	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
498	
499	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
500	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
501	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
502	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
503	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
504	                if not hypotheses:
505	                    # discover() surfaced nothing — a vacuous run indistinguishable from a broken
506	                    # callable that forgot to return. Fail loud (pass 3, #F-042).
507	                    raise FirewallViolation(
508	                        "discover() returned no hypotheses — a discovery run with zero "
509	                        "hypotheses is vacuous and cannot produce a confirmatory verdict"
510	                    )
511	                # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated hypothesis_id would be counted once
512	                # per occurrence in the verdict accounting (#138)
513	                _require_unique_ids([h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses], "hypothesis_id")
514	                trail.record(
515	                    "discovery",
516	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
517	                    n=len(hypotheses),
518	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
519	                )
520	
521	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
522	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
523	                if not held_out.units:
524	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
525	                preregister_instant = _now()
526	                for h in hypotheses:
527	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
528	                        raise FirewallViolation(
529	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
530	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
531	                        )
532	                    vt = require_preregistered(
533	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
534	                    )
535	                    trail.record(
536	                        "preregister",
537	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
538	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
539	                        digest=vt.digest,
540	                        locked_at=vt.instant.isoformat(),
541	                    )
542	                run_gate(
543	                    GateReview(
544	                        gate="review-locked-hypotheses",
545	                        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
546	                        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
547	                        summary="review locked hypotheses before confirmatory testing",
548	                        payload={
549	                            "hypothesis_ids": [h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
550	                            "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
551	                        },
552	                    )
553	                )
554	
555	            with tracer.span("phase:CONFIRM"):
556	                enter(Phase.CONFIRM)
557	                confirmed: list[Verdict] = []
558	                for h in hypotheses:
559	                    verdict = plan.confirm_held_out(h, held_out)  # only the held-out partition
560	                    # A confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; accessing
561	                    # verdict.hypothesis_id would raise an opaque AttributeError instead of the
562	                    # documented typed error (pass 3, #F-021). Guard the contract explicitly.
563	                    if not isinstance(verdict, Verdict):
564	                        raise FirewallViolation(
565	                            f"confirm_held_out returned {type(verdict).__name__} for hypothesis "
566	                            f"{h.hypothesis_id!r} — expected a Verdict"
567	                        )
568	                    if verdict.hypothesis_id != h.hypothesis_id:
569	                        raise FirewallViolation(
570	                            f"verdict reports hypothesis_id {verdict.hypothesis_id!r} for "
571	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} — hypothesis↔verdict misattribution"
572	                        )
573	                    confirmed.append(verdict)
574	                    trail.record(
575	                        "verdict",
576	                        f"confirmed {h.hypothesis_id!r}: {verdict.label.value}",
577	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
578	                        label=verdict.label.value,
579	                        rule=verdict.decision_rule,
580	                    )
581	                discovery_verdicts = tuple(confirmed)
582	                for lbl in ("supported", "contradicted", "indeterminate"):
583	                    n = sum(1 for v in discovery_verdicts if v.label.value == lbl)
584	                    track(_count_logger(f"discovery_{lbl}", float(n)))
585	
586	        # ---- ADJUDICATE → SCORE (Firewall A; the baseline built above is reused, blind) ----
587	        if StudyMode.ADJUDICATE_EXTERNAL_CLAIMS in modes:
588	            assert defn.claims_source is not None
589	            assert plan.hypothesis_for is not None and plan.confirm_claim is not None
590	            claim_records = list(defn.claims_source.claims())
591	            if not claim_records:
592	                raise ValueError(
593	                    "adjudication mode but the claims source yielded no claims — nothing to adjudicate"
594	                )
595	            # within-run identity uniqueness: a repeated claim_id would inflate the scorecard (#138)
596	            _require_unique_ids([c.claim_id for c in claim_records], "claim_id")
597	            track(_count_logger("n_claims", float(len(claim_records))))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
598	            # The engine fingerprints the EXACT claims it will score (authoritative), and records
599	            # the source's self-report separately for cross-check rather than trusting it (#137).
600	            engine_claim_fp = _engine_claim_fingerprint(claim_records)
601	            source_claim_fp = defn.claims_source.claim_fingerprint()
602	            # Enforce — do not merely *record* — the fingerprint agreement (pass 3, #F-001).
603	            # Pass-2 #137 made the engine fingerprint authoritative and surfaced the match as a
604	            # boolean in the gate payload, but a non-interactive gate (auto_approve) approves
605	            # regardless, so a source whose claim_fingerprint() lies about a claim set that
606	            # claims() does not actually yield completed a run with the discrepancy silently
607	            # recorded. The source's self-report is a content commitment for pre-registration;
608	            # a disagreement means the scored claims are not the set the source attested to —
609	            # a provenance-integrity breach the engine must refuse, not defer to a human.
610	            if source_claim_fp != engine_claim_fp:
611	                raise FirewallViolation(
612	                    "claims source self-reported claim_fingerprint "
613	                    f"{source_claim_fp!r} but the engine computed {engine_claim_fp!r} over the "
614	                    "claims actually yielded — the scored claim set does not match the source's "
615	                    "attested fingerprint (provenance-integrity breach)"
616	                )
617	            prev_phase = Phase.CONFIRM if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes else Phase.BASELINE
618	            run_gate(
619	                GateReview(
620	                    gate="review-baseline-and-claims",
621	                    frm=prev_phase,
622	                    to=Phase.ADJUDICATE,
623	                    summary="review the blind baseline and the external claim set before adjudication",
624	                    payload={
625	                        "n_claims": len(claim_records),
626	                        "claim_ids": [c.claim_id for c in claim_records],
627	                        "claim_fingerprint": engine_claim_fp,  # engine-computed over scored claims
628	                        "source_reported_claim_fingerprint": source_claim_fp,  # unverified self-report
629	                        "claim_fingerprint_matches_source": engine_claim_fp == source_claim_fp,
630	                        "baseline_fingerprint": baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
631	                        "research_questions": list(defn.research_questions),
632	                    },
633	                )
634	            )
635	            with tracer.span("phase:ADJUDICATE"):
636	                enter(Phase.ADJUDICATE)
637	
638	                def _record_adjudication_step(h: Hypothesis, v: Verdict) -> None:
639	                    trail.record(
640	                        "preregister",
641	                        f"locked claim hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
642	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,
643	                        source_claim_id=h.source_claim_id,
644	                        locked_at=h.locked_at,
645	                    )
646	                    trail.record(
647	                        "verdict",
648	                        f"adjudicated {v.hypothesis_id!r}: {v.label.value}",
649	                        hypothesis_id=v.hypothesis_id,
650	                        label=v.label.value,
651	                        rule=v.decision_rule,
652	                    )
653	
654	                # not_after = baseline_instant: a claim-derived hypothesis must have been locked
655	                # before the (shared) baseline existed. The SAME materialized claim_records that
656	                # were gated/recorded are scored (#95).
657	                scorecard = adjudicate_with_baseline(
658	                    baseline,
659	                    claim_records,
660	                    hypothesis_for=plan.hypothesis_for,
661	                    confirm=plan.confirm_claim,
662	                    authority=plan.authority,
663	                    not_after=baseline_instant,
664	                    source_name=defn.name,
665	                    on_step=_record_adjudication_step,
666	                )
667	            with tracer.span("phase:SCORE"):
668	                enter(Phase.SCORE)
669	                trail.record(
670	                    "score",
671	                    f"source scorecard for {defn.name!r}",
672	                    n_supported=scorecard.n_supported,
673	                    n_contradicted=scorecard.n_contradicted,
674	                    n_indeterminate=scorecard.n_indeterminate,
675	                    alignment_rate=scorecard.alignment_rate,
676	                )
677	                sc = scorecard
678	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_supported", float(sc.n_supported)))
679	                track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_contradicted", float(sc.n_contradicted)))
680	                track(
681	                    lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "n_indeterminate", float(sc.n_indeterminate))
682	                )
683	                ar = sc.alignment_rate
684	                if ar is not None:
685	                    track(lambda t, rid: t.log_metric(rid, "alignment_rate", float(ar)))
686	
687	        # ---- REPORT ----
688	        with tracer.span("phase:REPORT"):
689	            enter(Phase.REPORT)
690	            trail.record(
691	                "report",
692	                "assembled reproducibility package",
693	                n_discovery_verdicts=len(discovery_verdicts),
694	                has_scorecard=scorecard is not None,
695	            )
696	
697	        if tuple(visited) != required:
698	            raise FirewallViolation(
699	                f"visited phase sequence {[p.name for p in visited]} != required "
700	                f"{[p.name for p in required]}"
701	            )
702	        if verify_trail:
703	            # Re-verify the whole chain before handing back the result. This re-hashes every
704	            # entry — O(N) — which is redundant for an in-memory trail this call built and never
705	            # mutated (each entry was hashed correctly when appended). It is a cheap safety net in
706	            # the common case but a real cost for very large trails, so a perf-sensitive caller
707	            # that trusts the in-process trail may pass verify_trail=False (pass 3, #F-036). The
708	            # important re-verification — of a trail deserialized from an external store — is the
709	            # caller's verify_records() on from_records(), which this flag does not affect.
710	            trail.verify()  # the provenance chain must be intact before we hand back the result
711	        # Terminal success entry (pass 3, #F-006): a phase 'report' record is not a run-level
712	        # terminus, so a trail truncated mid-run is otherwise indistinguishable from one that
713	        # completed. Record run_end ONLY after verify() passes, so its presence as the last
714	        # entry is an affirmative "this run finished with an intact chain" marker. The payload is
715	        # deterministic (no wall-clock) so the provenance-hash determinism guarantee holds — the
716	        # non-deterministic run duration goes to the experiment tracker as an SLO metric instead.
717	        trail.record(
718	            "run_end",
719	            f"study {defn.name!r} completed successfully",
720	            study=defn.name,
721	            experiment_run_id=run_id,
722	            n_provenance_entries=len(trail.entries) + 1,  # +1 counts this terminal entry itself
723	        )
724	        track(_count_logger("run_duration_s", time.monotonic() - started_at))  # SLO metric (#F-029)
725	        ok = True
726	    except Exception as exc:
727	        # Record a terminal failure entry BEFORE re-raising so the abort reason is recoverable
728	        # from the (caller-owned) trail, not lost (#109).
729	        last_phase = visited[-1].name if visited else "start"
730	        try:
731	            trail.record(
732	                "run_failed",
733	                f"{type(exc).__name__} during {last_phase}",
734	                error_type=type(exc).__name__,
735	                message=str(exc)[:2000],
736	                last_phase=last_phase,
737	            )
738	        except Exception as inner:  # noqa: BLE001 — never mask the original failure
739	            # The run_failed entry could not be recorded (payload unserializable, clock raised,
740	            # trail lock contended). Don't silently swallow — emit a structured warning so the
741	            # failure reason is at least in the logs, even though it is absent from the trail
742	            # (pass 3, #F-030). The original exception is still re-raised below.
743	            _log.warning(
744	                "could not record run_failed provenance entry (%s: %s)",
745	                type(inner).__name__,
746	                inner,
747	            )
748	        raise
749	    finally:
750	        trace_ctx.close()
751	        if tracker is not None and run_id is not None:
752	            try:
753	                # a failed run must be distinguishable from a successful one in the store (#110)
754	                tracker.end_run(run_id, status="FINISHED" if ok else "FAILED")
755	            except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001,S110 — best-effort cleanup
756	                pass
757	
758	    return StudyResult(
759	        study=defn.name,
760	        corpus_fingerprint=cfp,
761	        baseline=baseline,
762	        discovery_verdicts=discovery_verdicts,
763	        scorecard=scorecard,
764	        phases=tuple(visited),
765	        provenance=trail.entries,
766	        source_version=source_version,
767	        feature_matrices=tuple(feature_matrices),
768	        experiment_run_id=run_id,
769	    )
770

content.text: That preview shows an older version of the test. Let me read the actual current test file.

content.content:
424	def test_tracker_receives_run_and_metrics_and_failures_dont_abort(tmp_path):
425	    class FakeTracker:
426	        def __init__(self):
427	            self.calls = []
428	
429	        def start_run(self, name, params):
430	            self.calls.append(("start", name))
431	            return "run-1"
432	
433	        def log_metric(self, run_id, key, value):
434	            self.calls.append(("metric", key, value))
435	
436	        def log_artifact(self, run_id, path):
437	            self.calls.append(("artifact", path))
438	
439	        def end_run(self, run_id, status="FINISHED"):
440	            self.calls.append(("end", run_id, status))
441	
442	    t = FakeTracker()
443	    res = _run(tmp_path, ALL, tracker=t)
444	    assert res.experiment_run_id == "run-1"
445	    assert ("start", "reference-study") in t.calls and ("end", "run-1", "FINISHED") in t.calls
446	    assert any(c[0] == "metric" and c[1] == "alignment_rate" for c in t.calls)
447	    # #F-029: SLO-relevant scale + latency metrics are logged so corpus-size↔latency regressions
448	    # are analysable from the experiment store, not just the provenance trail.
449	    metric_keys = {c[1] for c in t.calls if c[0] == "metric"}
450	    assert {"n_units", "n_claims", "run_duration_s"} <= metric_keys
451	
452	    class BoomTracker(FakeTracker):
453	        def log_metric(self, *a):
454	            raise RuntimeError("tracker down")
455	
456	    res2 = _run(tmp_path, ADJUDICATE, tracker=BoomTracker())  # must NOT abort the run
457	    assert res2.scorecard is not None
458	    assert any(e.kind == "tracking_error" for e in res2.provenance)
459	
460	
461	def test_versioner_failure_is_wrapped_as_ingestion_error(tmp_path):
462	    # #F-008: a storage failure in the optional versioner seam must surface as the documented
463	    # IngestionError, not a raw untyped exception the caller cannot distinguish from a firewall
464	    # breach. Pre-fix: versioner.put() was called bare and PermissionError propagated raw.
465	    from assay_engine.pipeline import IngestionError
466	
467	    class BoomVersioner:
468	        def put(self, ref_str):
469	            raise PermissionError("read-only store")
470	
471	        def path_for(self, version):  # pragma: no cover - not reached
472	            raise AssertionError
473	
474	    with pytest.raises(IngestionError, match="data versioner failed"):
475	        _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY, versioner=BoomVersioner())
476	
477	
478	def test_confirm_held_out_returning_none_raises_firewall_violation(tmp_path):
479	    # #F-021: a confirmer that forgets to `return` yields None; the runner must raise a typed
480	    # FirewallViolation, not die on an opaque AttributeError at verdict.hypothesis_id.
481	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
482	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="expected a Verdict"):
483	        run_study(replace(plan, confirm_held_out=lambda h, c: None), gate_handler=auto_approve)
484	
485	
486	def test_empty_discover_in_pipeline_raises(tmp_path):
487	    # #F-042: a discover() returning no hypotheses is a vacuous run; the pipeline must fail loud
488	    # rather than complete with zero verdicts (indistinguishable from a broken callable).
489	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=DISCOVERY)
490	    with pytest.raises(FirewallViolation, match="no corpus units|no hypotheses|discover"):
491	        run_study(replace(plan, discover=lambda c: []), gate_handler=auto_approve)
492	
493

content.text: The test asserts exactly the 3 metric keys. Let me verify `ALL` mode covers all three (n_units always, n_claims requires adjudication, run_duration_s on success) and confirm the test runs.
`ALL = frozenset(StudyMode)` includes adjudication, so n_claims is logged. n_units is always logged in INGEST, run_duration_s on success. Let me confirm the test passes.

Each metric is logged exactly once via `track()`. No duplicate path could mask a revert. The test's subset assertion would fail if any of the three were removed. The BoomTracker sub-case also confirms the best-effort contract (tracker failure doesn't abort, records `tracking_error`).

All three SLO metric keys are asserted, the test passes, it discriminates the fix (reverting any of the three `track()` calls drops a key and fails the subset assertion), and the change is idiomatic (reuses the existing best-effort `track()`/`_count_logger` helpers, n_units in INGEST, n_claims in ADJUDICATE, run_duration_s only on success). No regression.
